---
title: Human Cell Atlas (HCA)
alias: &ALIAS humancellatlas
href: https://www.humancellatlas.org/
listing:
  id: pubs
  template: ../../../../_ejs/publications-people.ejs 
  contents: 
    - "../../../../publications/**/*.qmd"
    - "!../../../../publications/_template/"
  sort: "pub_number desc"
  include:
    categories: *ALIAS
  fields: [publication, title, categories, image, date, author]

---

## Overview

The Human Cell Atlas (HCA) is an international consortium building a comprehensive reference atlas of all human cells to understand the foundation of human health and disease.

## Key Features

- Generates single-cell and spatial transcriptomic data across all major human tissues and organs
- Provides open-access data through a standardized Data Portal with harmonized metadata
- Enables cell type identification, annotation, and cross-tissue comparison at single-cell resolution
- Integrates multimodal data including RNA-seq, ATAC-seq, and spatial transcriptomics
- Supports community-developed pipelines for data processing, QC, and interoperability

## Use Cases in Research

- Identifying host cell types and states targeted by pathogens during infectious disease
- Building reference atlases of immune cell populations relevant to AMR and infection response
- Comparing healthy vs. diseased cell states in tissues affected by infection or inflammation
- Training AI/ML foundation models on large-scale single-cell gene expression data
- Annotating novel cell types discovered in microbiome-host interaction studies

## Getting Started

Visit [Human Cell Atlas (HCA)](https://www.humancellatlas.org/) to access the database.

## Related Publications

:::{#pubs}
:::
